_comment_: Seed config consumed by infrastructure/uptime-kuma/seed.py. Read top-to-bottom: notifications first (referenced by name from monitors), then monitors, then the public status page that surfaces them. Notification names + monitor names are the idempotency keys — re-running the seed updates fields in place on a name match, never duplicates.

notifications.name: Discord (#uptime)
notifications.comment: Native Discord webhook (Kuma renders Discord-native embeds — posting Slack format to a Discord URL fails as an empty message). Uses its own DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL, separate from the aiops-webhook's #alerts channel. When the env var is empty, seed.py skips creation entirely (the demo still works without a Discord server).

notifications.name: Email (oncall)
notifications.comment: Posts to the Mailpit SMTP catcher (mailpit:1025). Captured in the Mailpit UI and, in the cluster, relayed out to a real inbox via Resend. Locally visible at http://localhost:8025.

monitors.name: Frontend (Next.js)
monitors.comment: Next.js standalone often returns 307 from / when the auth-guard redirects to /dashboard — accept 3xx so the redirect doesn't read as DOWN.

monitors.name: Backend — live
monitors.comment: Liveness probe — returns 200 if the ASP.NET host is responsive, no downstream checks. See docs/operations/health-probes.md.

monitors.name: Backend — ready
monitors.comment: Readiness probe — 200 only when Postgres is reachable. 503 means the API would refuse traffic in K8s.

monitors.name: Backend — diagnostics
monitors.comment: Full downstream picture — Postgres + Redis + RabbitMQ + Keycloak JWKS. A 503 here surfaces a brittle dependency before the readiness probe ever fires.

monitors.name: Keycloak — realm well-known
monitors.comment: OIDC discovery endpoint — also gates Keycloak's own healthcheck. DOWN here means tokens won't issue.

monitors.name: Postgres
monitors.comment: Connects via the same connection string the backend uses; runs SELECT 1 every minute. Credentials come from env (${POSTGRES_*}, injected from the myproperty-postgres Secret in K8s); falls back to the compose dev defaults when unset.

monitors.name: Redis
monitors.comment: Redis PING probe. Password comes from ${REDIS_PASSWORD} (myproperty-redis Secret in K8s); empty in compose dev, where Redis runs auth-less and the empty userinfo sends no AUTH. The API tolerates brief Redis outages by design (cache + SignalR backplane fall back gracefully) — this monitor exists for visibility, not for paging.

monitors.name: RabbitMQ — management
monitors.comment: Rabbit's built-in health/checks/alarms endpoint — green when there are no memory or disk alarms firing on any node. Basic auth creds come from env (${RABBITMQ_USER}/${RABBITMQ_PASSWORD}, injected from the myproperty-rabbitmq Secret in K8s); falls back to the compose dev guest/guest when unset.

monitors.name: AIOps webhook
monitors.comment: Closes the loop: if the AIOps triage service goes dark, this monitor alerts via Discord/email even though the AIOps webhook itself can't.

monitors.name: Unleash (feature flags)
monitors.comment: Unleash built-in /health endpoint (returns {"health":"GOOD"}). Non-critical: the backend defaults flags ON when Unleash is unreachable, so an outage is otherwise silent — this monitor surfaces it (e.g. the missing-`unleash`-database crashloop) before a flag flip mysteriously has no effect. Kuma reaches it via the allow-uptime-kuma-probe catch-all NetworkPolicy.

monitors.name: Mailpit
monitors.comment: Mailpit liveness endpoint. DOWN means invite / password-reset / payment emails are dropping (the SMTP endpoint for backend + Keycloak). Kuma reaches it via the allow-uptime-kuma-probe catch-all NetworkPolicy.

statusPage.title: MyProperty — System Status
statusPage.description: Public health overview of MyProperty services. Updates every minute. Subscribe to the per-monitor RSS feeds for incident notifications.
